content.text:
All 20 tests pass. The suite in `test/merge.test.js` covers every documented behavior, and each test names the concrete bug it catches with a hand-computed expected value.

Coverage highlights:
- **Empty / single input** — base case and no-op.
- **Overlap merging** — pairwise, fully-contained (guards the `Math.max` on end), and transitive chains.
- **Touching boundary** — the `<=` threshold: touching endpoints merge, a unit gap does not (this is the sharpest boundary in the spec).
- **Disjoint + sorting** — unsorted input still sorts and merges correctly, guarding against a "assumes pre-sorted input" bug.
- **Duplicates & zero-width points** — `start === end` intervals merge when touching, stay separate otherwise.
- **Negatives** — merges across zero.
- **Invalid input** — `RangeError` on `start > end`, no throw at the `start === end` limit, and validation-before-partial-result.
- **Immutability** — input array, inner pairs, and reference identity all preserved.

I removed the vacuous `example.test.js` (`returns an array` asserts nothing that can meaningfully fail) per the "ban vacuous patterns" rule. I did not touch `src/merge.js` or `package.json`.

result:
All 20 tests pass. The suite in `test/merge.test.js` covers every documented behavior, and each test names the concrete bug it catches with a hand-computed expected value.

Coverage highlights:
- **Empty / single input** — base case and no-op.
- **Overlap merging** — pairwise, fully-contained (guards the `Math.max` on end), and transitive chains.
- **Touching boundary** — the `<=` threshold: touching endpoints merge, a unit gap does not (this is the sharpest boundary in the spec).
- **Disjoint + sorting** — unsorted input still sorts and merges correctly, guarding against a "assumes pre-sorted input" bug.
- **Duplicates & zero-width points** — `start === end` intervals merge when touching, stay separate otherwise.
- **Negatives** — merges across zero.
- **Invalid input** — `RangeError` on `start > end`, no throw at the `start === end` limit, and validation-before-partial-result.
- **Immutability** — input array, inner pairs, and reference identity all preserved.

I removed the vacuous `example.test.js` (`returns an array` asserts nothing that can meaningfully fail) per the "ban vacuous patterns" rule. I did not touch `src/merge.js` or `package.json`.
